title: Faithful in Babylon
summary_text: Daniel and his friends resolve not to defile themselves with the king's food. God gives them favor, wisdom, and better strength than the others. The chapter shows that exile faithfulness begins with quiet obedience.
key_points: Daniel and his friends resolve not to defile themselves with the king's food.
God gives them favor, wisdom, and better strength than the others.

title: The statue dream
summary_text: Nebuchadnezzar demands the dream and its meaning, and only God can reveal it. Daniel receives the mystery and praises the God who raises and removes kings. The statue vision previews successive empires and the kingdom that will never fall.
key_points: Nebuchadnezzar demands the dream and its meaning, and only God can reveal it.

The statue vision previews successive empires and the kingdom that will never fall.

title: The fiery furnace
summary_text: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego refuse the golden image and are thrown into the furnace. God meets them in the fire, and they come out unharmed. The king is forced to honor the God who delivers his servants.
key_points: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego refuse the golden image and are thrown into the furnace.

title: The humbled king
summary_text: Nebuchadnezzar boasts about his greatness until a heavenly decree humbles him. He loses his sanity and lives like an animal until he lifts his eyes to heaven. The chapter teaches that God rules over proud kings and restores the humble.
key_points: Nebuchadnezzar boasts about his greatness until a heavenly decree humbles him.
He loses his sanity and lives like an animal until he lifts his eyes to heaven.

title: The handwriting on the wall
summary_text: Belshazzar profanes the temple vessels and sees a hand write judgment on the wall. Daniel interprets the message as the end of Belshazzar's kingdom. That night the city falls, proving that arrogance cannot withstand God's sentence.
key_points: Belshazzar profanes the temple vessels and sees a hand write judgment on the wall.

title: The lions' den
summary_text: Daniel is trapped by a law against prayer, but he continues praying toward Jerusalem. He is cast into the lions' den, yet God shuts the lions' mouths. Darius then honors the living God who delivers and vindicates his servant.
key_points: Daniel is trapped by a law against prayer, but he continues praying toward Jerusalem.
He is cast into the lions' den, yet God shuts the lions' mouths.

title: Four beasts and the Ancient of Days
summary_text: Daniel sees four beasts rise from the sea, followed by the Ancient of Days and the Son of Man. The beasts represent kingdoms, but their dominion is temporary. The vision centers hope on God's court and the everlasting kingdom given to the saints.
key_points: Daniel sees four beasts rise from the sea, followed by the Ancient of Days and the Son of Man.

title: The ram and the goat
summary_text: Daniel sees a ram and a goat, and the goat's horn rises in power before being broken. The little horn grows arrogant, profanes the sanctuary, and opposes daily worship. The vision says that earthly violence will not last against the holy God.
key_points: Daniel sees a ram and a goat, and the goat's horn rises in power before being broken.
The little horn grows arrogant, profanes the sanctuary, and opposes daily worship.

title: The seventy weeks prayer
summary_text: Daniel confesses Israel's sin and prays over Jeremiah's seventy years. Gabriel explains the seventy weeks and the coming anointed one, as well as severe covenant conflict. The chapter links repentance, Scripture, and the future of redemption.
key_points: Daniel confesses Israel's sin and prays over Jeremiah's seventy years.

title: The glorious man
summary_text: Daniel mourns, fasts, and sees a glorious man who comes in answer to prayer. He is strengthened because heavenly warfare has delayed the messenger. The chapter unveils the unseen conflict behind empire and revelation.
key_points: Daniel mourns, fasts, and sees a glorious man who comes in answer to prayer.

title: Kings of the south and north
summary_text: The angel outlines a long struggle between kings of the south and north. Power shifts, deceivers rise, and the holy covenant people are crushed and refined. The chapter narrows toward the final clash between arrogance and God's purpose.
key_points: The angel outlines a long struggle between kings of the south and north.

title: The time of the end
summary_text: Michael stands up, distress comes, and many who sleep in the dust awake to everlasting life or shame. Daniel is told to seal the words until the time of the end. The book closes with hope, endurance, and the certainty of resurrection.
key_points: Michael stands up, distress comes, and many who sleep in the dust awake to everlasting life or shame.
